subject: Welcome to D.A.P.A.U.E.
body:
Hi David,

Welcome to the Archives team. We are delighted to have you with us.

Attached is the orientation file and the employee handbook. Please note that the canteen on Level 2 is currently closed for 'biological decontamination' (a blocked drain, I'm told!), so stick to the vending machines on Level 1 for now.

Do let me know if you need anything more.

Regards,
Kara
HR Coordinator

subject: Security Notice: Mandatory Reading
body:
Mr. Parker,

Please review the attached security guidelines immediately. 

Pay particular attention to Section 4: **Data Sovereignty and the Non-Disclosure of Trial Participant Status**. 

You are working with sensitive biological data. What you see on the screen stays on the screen. Do not discuss file contents in the break room, the lifts, or outside the building. Nauralink recording is strictly prohibited within the facility.

Confirm receipt by replying to this automated message.

subject: Hello from the front desk
body:
Hi Michael,

David here—the new guy who spilled coffee near the scanners this morning. Thanks for helping me clean that up without alerting the guards. 

Just settling in at the Archives desk. It's a bit quiet down here, isn't it? If you fancy a coffee (that I promise not to spill) later, let me know. 

Cheers,
David

subject: RE: Hello from the front desk
body:
David!

Don't worry about it. Security Manager King would have had a field day if he saw that stain on the carpet. 

It is quiet in Archives. Too quiet if you ask me. You'll get used to the hum of the servers eventually. I'm stuck at the desk until 5 (big delegation coming in from the Ministry), but pop by on your way out. 

Welcome to the madhouse.

- M

subject: Morning, New Guy
body:
David, 

Welcome aboard. You're taking over the Batch 400 series logs. 

Don't worry about the deletions in the system—some files are 'scrubbed' by Management for legal shielding. It happens. Just focus on the raw data entry for the new intakes. 

If you see anything odd—corrupted dates, mismatched names, etc.—just flag it to me first, alright? Don't put it in the official ticket system. It saves us the paperwork.

- Mitchell
Records Archivist

subject: Question regarding subject gait/movement
body:
Hi Amanda,

I was in the lobby earlier and saw one of the trial participants (Subject ID tag ended in 44). They were walking towards the exit, but every third step, their left leg seemed to lock up mechanically. It looked painful.

I couldn't find a bug report for this in the system to cross-reference with my data entry. Should I log a maintenance request for their hardware?

Thanks,
David Parker

subject: RE: Question regarding subject gait/movement
body:
David, 

I heard you asking about the 'mechanical hitch' in the lobby. 

We call them 'Limpers' in Scenario Dev. It’s just a minor hardware glitch, nothing for Data Entry to worry about. It's essentially a lag in the translation layer between the user's intent and the servo-motor.

Try not to stare next time; it makes the subjects self-conscious. And please, do not log maintenance requests. The Scenario team is aware of the parameters.

Amanda

subject: Weird vibe today
body:
Mike,

Did you see the reply I got from White in Scenario Dev? 'Limpers'? Seems a bit derogatory for a medical facility.

Also, that guy I saw... he didn't look self-conscious. He looked terrified. He was trying to run, but his leg wouldn't let him do anything faster than a shuffle. Is that normal?

- D

subject: RE: Weird vibe today
body:
David,

Keep your voice down, digitally speaking. White has a morbid sense of humour, everyone knows that.

But yeah, I see them pass the desk every day. The 'Limpers' are the ones who usually don't come back for their check-ups. I overheard Security saying something about 'No-Go Zones' last week. Just... focus on your spreadsheets, mate. We're going for a pint on Friday, yeah? You need to relax.

- M

subject: Private: Question about the trials
body:
David, 

I know you're new and maybe I shouldn't be asking, but I'm a bit worried. 

My brother is in a Class-B trial. His bionic arm is starting to feel 'heavy'—he says it's like the AI is trying to move for him before he even thinks it. He was pouring tea and the arm jerked and threw the cup across the room before he even commanded the muscle.

You see the logs for the neural maps, right? Is that normal? Please don't tell anyone I asked, especially not Taylor or Martin.

- Emily

subject: Log Discrepancy: Batch 402
body:
Dear Mr. Taylor, 

I was reconciling the Batch 402 logs this afternoon and noticed a conflict I can't resolve.

Subject #992 is listed as 'Withdrawn - Personal Reasons' on the public ledger, but the internal retrieval log shows a 'Class-B Post-Mortem Recovery' on the exact same timestamp. 

I'm unable to edit this discrepancy to match the public ledger. Is there a specific protocol for 'Rapid Harvest' cases I should be using to clear this error?

Regards,
David Parker

subject: RE: Log Discrepancy: Batch 402
body:
Mr. Parker, 

Administrative errors are to be reported via the automated ticket system, not my direct inbox. 

The subject in question was a 'Write-off' under Class-C protocol. The 'Rapid Harvest' tag is an automated legacy error in the database schema. Ignore it.

Do not attempt to access those medical records again. Your clearance level does not cover post-mortem summaries or asset reclamation details.

Focus on your assigned batches.

- Rob
Operations Coordinator

subject: She's charming, isn't she?
body:
Just got my head bitten off by Taylor for pointing out a data error. Apparently, dead people are just 'legacy errors' in the database.

I'm telling you Mike, the numbers don't add up. We have more 'Write-offs' than we have failed trials. Where are the organs going? I saw a manifest for 'Biological Waste' leaving the loading bay at 3 AM on the security logs (I clicked the wrong folder, honest).

Meet me for lunch? I need to get out of this room.

- D

subject: Class-A Retrieval tonight
body:
Hey David, 

I know you're new to the archive desk, but Mitchell is out sick.

I've got a Class-A extraction scheduled for tonight in the slums (Sector 4). The mercenaries are being difficult about the 'data chip' integrity. They want to know if they can just smash the skull or if they need to do a surgical removal.

Can you send over the latest neural map for Target #402-Alpha? It makes the harvest much cleaner if we know where the AI nested the consciousness backup. 

Need this ASAP before the team deploys.

- Kevin Brown
Medical Tech

subject: RE: Class-A Retrieval tonight
body:
Kevin,

I... I don't think I have access to that. And surely we shouldn't be discussing "smashing skulls" via email? 

I can see a file for #402-Alpha, but it's flagged as a human being, a Mrs. Sarah Jenkins. Is she not coming in for a voluntary removal? The file says she's just late for an appointment.

I'm forwarding this request to Mr. Allen in Medical Ops, as I'm not comfortable releasing neural maps for an involuntary extraction.

- David

subject: DELIVERY FAILURE: RE: Class-A Retrieval tonight
body:
Your message to 'D_ALLEN' was blocked by the content filter.

Reason: Use of flagged terminology ('involuntary extraction', 'human being'). 

This incident has been logged and forwarded to Compliance (B_MARTIN).

subject: The Bus Stop
body:
Chen, 

Following up on our chat in the break room.

Have you noticed how many people just... stop... at the bus stop outside? I checked the timing from my window. It's exactly four minutes. Every time. They look like they're sleeping on their feet, total zoning out.

Is there a collection node hidden there? I keep finding these 'Data Bursts' in the traffic logs that match the bus schedule perfectly. It's like the AI is forcing them to stand still so it can upload their brain maps to the server.

If your brother feels 'heavy', tell him to avoid the bus stop on 4th Street.

- D

subject: Compliance Warning #1
body:
Mr. Parker,

It has come to my attention that you have been querying 'Collection Nodes' and 'Social Engineering' protocols in your correspondence.

These are classified Executive-level strategic assets. You were hired for data entry, not system analysis.

We monitor keywords for the safety of our proprietary technology. This is your first and final warning regarding 'curiosity' in the archives. 

Stick to the spreadsheets, Mr. Parker. We would hate to have to review your employment contract so soon.

- Briana Martin
Compliance Officer

subject: DRAFT: Resignation
body:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to formally resign from my position as Data Entry Clerk, effective immediately.

I cannot in good conscience continue to work for an organisation that treats human life as hardware. I have seen the emails. I know about the harvests. I know about the 'write-offs'.

You are killing people.

subject: Off-the-record: Enzyme Anomalies
body:
David, 

I saw your query on the shared drive before Martin scrubbed it. You're onto something.

I think I've found a pattern. The biological rejection isn't random. The enzyme levels spike exactly 48 hours after they go near the 'firewall' areas in the tech-slums. It's like the body is reacting to the AI trying to 're-sync' the brain map against a jammed signal.

If they're using placebos (which 80% of them are), these people are basically walking towards a coma. The 'infection' is the AI itself.

Delete this after reading.

- Robinson

subject: Wellness Check-in
body:
David, 

I noticed you've been quite stressed lately. Your keystroke cadence has changed significantly in the last 48 hours, and you're spending longer than average on the 'Deceased Assets' files.

We take staff mental health very seriously at DAPAUE. 

Why don't you pop by my office for a chat tomorrow morning? Let's talk about 'organisational malaise' and get you back on track. It's not a request.

- Dr. Paul Jackson
Staff Psychologist

subject: URGENT - I'm being followed
body:
Mitchell, 

I know you're back on Monday but read this as soon as you login.

Someone has been in my desk. My blue notebook is gone. It had all my notes on the Class-A extraction orders and the bus stop timings.

I’m scared. I think someone is watching my screen through the Neuralink. My cursor keeps drifting to the 'Delete' button on its own. 

If I don't come in tomorrow, please check the Archive 7 backup tape in the basement. I hid something there. A copy of the Sarah Jenkins file.

Trust no one, especially Jackson.

- David

subject: Mandatory Meeting: Office of the Director
body:
Mr. Parker, 

Please report to the Chief of Staff's office immediately for a mandatory security briefing regarding your unauthorized access of Archive 7. 

Do not shut down your computer. Do not bring your personal devices. 

A security escort has been dispatched to your workstation to assist you.

- Mrs. Susan Walker
Chief of Staff
